methods.description: Process for obtaining protein sequences, performing multiple sequence alignment, identifying domains and signals, and constructing a phylogenetic tree to study evolutionary relationships of C2H2 Zinc Finger Proteins (ZFPs).

steps.description: C2H2 ZFP sequences were obtained from the NCBI database (https://www.ncbi.nlm. nih.gov/ accessed on 6 April 2015).
steps.searchable_summary: download protein sequences from NCBI database

steps.description: Multiple protein sequence alignments were done by using the MEGA X software (version 11.0) [48] with ClustalW, and default parameters [49].
steps.searchable_summary: perform multiple protein sequence alignment using MEGA X and ClustalW

steps.description: For the alignment edition, we used BioEdit (version 7.2) [50].
steps.searchable_summary: edit multiple sequence alignment using BioEdit

steps.description: The identification of the C2H2 Zinc finger domains was performed in InterProScan (https://www.ebi.ac.uk/interpro/ search/sequence/ accessed on 21 April 2015) [51].
outputs: Protein domain predictions (e.g., coordinates, domain type)

steps.description: The nuclear localization signal was determined in NLStradamus (http://www.moseslab.csb.utoronto.ca/NLStradamus/ accessed on 21 April 2015) [47].
steps.searchable_summary: predict nuclear localization signal using NLStradamus

steps.description: Phylogenetic analysis was performed with MEGA X software (version 11.0 Philadelphia, USA) [48] by the Neighbor-Joining method [53], and the bootstrap resampling method using 1000 replicates [54].
steps.searchable_summary: construct phylogenetic tree using Neighbor-Joining method with bootstrap in MEGA X

methods.description: Method to determine the subcellular location of a protein (AtZAT4) by transiently expressing a fluorescent protein fusion (AtZAT4:sGFP) in plant cells (onion epidermal cells) via bombardment transformation and observing the fluorescence signal.

steps.description: transiently expressed a carboxyl-terminal fusion of AtZAT4 to the green fluorescent protein (GFP) under the control of CaMV 35S promoter
steps.searchable_summary: construct expression vector for protein-GFP fusion under CaMV 35S promoter

steps.description: transiently expressed ... in onion epidermal cells using the bombardment transformation method
steps.searchable_summary: perform transient transformation of plant cells using gene bombardment
outputs: Transformed onion epidermal cells expressing fusion protein

steps.description: The signal of AtZAT4:sGFP was observed only in the nucleus, while the control showed a GFP signal in both the nucleus and cytoplasm.
steps.searchable_summary: observe and image fluorescence signal in transformed cells using microscopy

methods.description: Method to measure the transcript levels of a specific gene (AtZAT4) in different plant tissues and developmental stages using reverse transcription-quantitative real-time PCR (RT-qPCR).

steps.description: proceeded to measure the level of transcripts using reverse transcription-quantitative real-time PCR (RT-qPCR) in 5-and 10-day-old seedlings and in different A. thaliana vegetative and reproductive tissues.
steps.searchable_summary: collect plant tissue samples for RNA extraction
inputs: Arabidopsis thaliana plants (various ages and tissues)

steps.description: proceeded to measure the level of transcripts using reverse transcription-quantitative real-time PCR (RT-qPCR)
steps.searchable_summary: perform RT-qPCR to measure gene transcript levels
inputs: RT-qPCR primers (for AtZAT4 and reference gene AtF-box)

steps.description: The constituti expression of the gene AtF-box [32] ... were used for normalization.
steps.searchable_summary: normalize RT-qPCR data using a reference gene (AtF-box)
inputs: Raw Ct values for target gene (AtZAT4) and reference gene (AtF-box)

steps.description: the 2 −ΔΔCt method described by Livak and Schmittgen (200 [33] were used for normalization.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate relative gene expression using the 2^-ΔΔCt method
inputs: Normalized Ct values (ΔCt) for samples and calibrator

steps.description: Data correspond to the mean  SE.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean and standard error of relative expression data
inputs: Relative transcript levels from multiple biological/technical replicates

steps.description: Significant differences (p ≤ 0.05) are shown with differe letters.
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical test to determine significant differences
outputs: Statistical test results (e.g., p-values, significance letters)

methods.description: Method to visualize gene expression patterns in different plant tissues and developmental stages using publicly available online databases like the eFP-browser.

steps.description: visualized in silico in the eFP-browser (http://bar.utoronto.ca/efp/cgi-bin/efpWeb.cgi, accessed on 21 April 2015)
visualized in silico in the eFP-browser ... in which AtZAT4 showed high levels of expression in mature pollen and stamens, and also in seeds.
steps.searchable_summary: search for gene and visualize expression profile in eFP-browser
outputs: Gene expression visualization (e.g., heat map, bar chart)
